summary: Tested VCF (vCard) import functionality added alongside CSV import. All 22 backend tests passed covering VCF preview endpoint, auto-detect endpoint, field parsing, phone priority, duplicate detection, and import confirmation.
critical_code_review_comments: VCF parser correctly extracts N: field into first_name/last_name
Phone label priority works correctly: Mobile > Cell > Main > Work > Home > Other, with 'pref' as fallback
Apple VCF format with 'item1.' prefix is handled by stripping the prefix
Multiple phones and emails are stored in phones[] and emails[] arrays with labels
ORG, TITLE, BDAY, ADR, NOTE fields all parse correctly
Birthday handles various formats including 'value=date:' prefix
Address parsing combines PO Box and Street into address_street
Auto-detect endpoint correctly routes .vcf/.vcard to VCF parser and .csv to CSV parser
Contacts without phone AND email are correctly skipped
Duplicate detection by last 7 digits of phone works for VCF imports
Import confirm endpoint sets ownership_type='personal', source='csv', tags=['csv-import']
seed_data_creation: Tests use inline VCF strings for most tests. Real VCF file test uses provided URL (1208 Apple vCard contacts). Test contacts are cleaned up after test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: VCF import feature fully tested. Test file test_vcf_import.py covers: VCF preview endpoint (all field parsing, phone/email arrays, organization, title, birthday, address, notes), phone label priority (mobile > work > home > pref fallback), auto-detect endpoint (routes .vcf to VCF parser, .csv to CSV parser), duplicate detection, import confirm (ownership_type, source, tags). Real VCF file with 1208 contacts parsed successfully.
